title: lygodium volubile var. acuminatum
intro: Lygodium volubile var. acuminatum, also known as the climbing fern, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Lygodiaceae and is native to Australia and New Guinea. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits associated with Lygodium volubile var. acuminatum include relief from fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for herbal use.
